general: Small and exotic pets require species-specific care. Research your specific animal's needs. Key daily tasks include feeding, fresh water, habitat cleaning, and health monitoring.
puppy_kitten: Young animals need more frequent feeding (2-4x daily), gentle handling for socialization, and species-appropriate enrichment. Consult a specialized vet early for vaccination or health guidance.
adult: Adult small pets need consistent feeding schedules (1-2x daily), weekly habitat cleaning, daily spot cleaning, and regular health checks. Handle regularly to maintain tameness.
senior: Older small pets need softer foods, easier habitat access, more frequent vet check-ups, and careful temperature regulation. Watch for signs of pain or discomfort.
feeding: Feed species-appropriate diet twice daily. Remove uneaten fresh food within 2-4 hours to prevent spoilage. Always provide fresh water; use a bottle or heavy bowl depending on species. Supplement with species-appropriate treats sparingly.
exercise: Provide daily out-of-habitat time if safe and appropriate for the species (e.g., rabbits, guinea pigs). Enrich the habitat with tunnels, toys, and climbing structures. Rotate enrichment items weekly.
grooming: Brush fur weekly if applicable. Trim nails every 3-4 weeks. Clean habitat weekly with full disinfection monthly. Monitor teeth, eyes, and coat condition during daily interactions.
vet_checkups: Annual wellness exam with an exotic animal or small mammal specialist. Many small pets hide illness well — watch for changes in appetite, activity, weight, or droppings. Emergency vet access is important as small pets can decline rapidly.
